messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Treat executable checks as the authority.

Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). If state.cooldown is above zero, reduce it by one and return false. If it is zero, set state.cooldown to input.cooldown, add one to state.actions, and return true.

meta.expected_behavior: Same branch contract as cooldown_gate, phrased differently.

messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Treat strings as atomic values unless explicitly asked to iterate characters.

Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). If all input.required flags exist in state.flags, add input.reward once to state.rewards and return true. If any flag is absent, leave state.rewards unchanged and return false.

meta.expected_behavior: Reward is one string value and must not be split into characters.

messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Keep state unchanged when the prompt says do not mutate state.

Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). Scan input.options in order, summing weight. Return the id from the first option whose cumulative weight is greater than input.roll. If the roll is beyond all cumulative weights, return the last option id. Do not change state.

meta.expected_behavior: Return option ids from cumulative weights, not indexes or sentinel values.
